question.text: An InvIT has a post-issue capital of Rs. 2,500 crore calculated at offer price. What is the minimum value of units that must be offered and allotted to the public?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since the post-issue capital (Rs. 2,500 crore) is equal to or more than Rs. 1,600 crore and less than Rs. 4,000 crore, the minimum offer to public shall be of the value of atleast Rs. 400 crore as per InvIT Regulations. **The Trap:** 25% applies only when post-issue capital is less than Rs. 1,600 crore. 10% applies when it is Rs. 4,000 crore or more. The Rs. 400 crore flat amount applies in the middle tier. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Three tiers for minimum public offer: <Rs. 1,600 crore = 25%; Rs. 1,600-4,000 crore = Rs. 400 crore; >=Rs. 4,000 crore = 10%. This is identical to REIT public offer thresholds.
